one_time_description: Large deferred revenue for Capital School Construction ($11,956,669) and significant ISC funding spike to $44,719,313 vs $16,603,297 prior year, driven by major capital projects (school construction and nursing station)
economic_notes: Nation has equity investments in Wasaya Group (49%) and Wasaya Partnership (51%) accounted for by modified equity method. Pe-Tay-Ka-Win Development Corporation and its subsidiary companies are consolidated. CMHC approved $1,052,000 loan for 4-unit housing project construction.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (March 31, 2020). Major capital projects drove large ISC funding increase: school construction ($18M in progress) and nursing station completed. Deferred revenue nearly doubled to $16.5M primarily due to Capital School Construction funding. Wasaya Group (49%) and Wasaya Partnership (51%) included via modified equity method using December 31, 2019 financials. No schedule of remuneration included in the statements provided. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($44,719,313) + Health Canada ($9,688,635) + CMHC ($254,048 per statement — note: $250,952 in revenue line but $254,048 in segment; used $250,952 from income statement) + Canada Post ($495,660) + Provincial ($570045) + SLAM Board ($160,873) + Ontario First Nation LLP ($1,492,925) — Canada Post and OFN LLP may be quasi-government; OSR estimated from Other revenue and Local revenue lines. COVID-19 note disclosed but no material financial impact quantified.
